summary: Tight soundboard capture of a scorching 1984 Cal Expo show with blistering China > Rider, The Other One and soaring Birdsong.
review: The SBD source is clean and well‑balanced, though a few listeners note a slight speed/tempo lift. Musically the concert is hailed as one of ’84’s highlights – Jerry’s solo work is described as creative and relentless, Brent’s keyboards shimmer, Phil locks in with Lesh, and the second set opens with an unforgettable China > Rider that drives the band into high‑octane jams on The Other One, Birdsong and He’s Gone. The only blemish mentioned is the “Don't Need Love” segment, which some find tedious.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly enthusiastic reviews (most 5‑star) praise both performance and sound quality; minor speed complaints and a single mixed‑rating review keep the score just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Well‑balanced, tight mix with crisp detail. A few listeners note the recording sounds slightly sped up, but overall clarity is high and no major drop‑outs are reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set sizzles with strong interplay; the second set launches with a memorable China > Rider and sustains high energy through extended jams on The Other One, Birdsong and He’s Gone.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Creative, relentless solos praised as "outstanding" and "blazing", though one reviewer called this specific performance sloppy.
band_member_comments.Phil: Locked in with Lesh, providing a solid bass foundation; noted for “plundering” the groove.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s 1984 keyboard work highlighted as distinctive, trippy and technically excellent throughout the jam sections.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum break is referenced positively (pre‑drums song), no specific complaints about Bill's playing.
